description_long: This is a rustic, round cabin with a steeply sloped, thatched roof that extends nearly to the ground, giving it a low, organic profile. The roof is constructed from natural materials, likely straw or reeds, and features a simple wooden ridge cap with visible crossbeams at the peak. The entrance is a small, arched opening supported by wooden posts, with no visible windows or additional doors. The exterior surface is textured and uneven, with patches of moss or weathering, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The structure appears compact and insulated, suitable for traditional or rural settings. Its earthy tones and natural materials blend seamlessly with outdoor environments.
description: A round, thatched-roof cabin with a steep, conical profile and a small arched entrance, constructed from natural materials and lacking visible windows.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main arched entrance supported by wooden posts and the prominent thatched roof with a wooden ridge cap.
description_view_1: This angled view highlights the thick, sloping thatched roof and the entrance partially visible on the left side.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the uninterrupted thatched roof surface, with no visible openings or architectural features.
description_view_3: This side view emphasizes the roof's conical shape and the wooden ridge cap, with the entrance just out of sight.
attribute_roof_type: steep conical thatched roof with a wooden ridge cap and visible crossbeams
attribute_window_arrangement: No visible windows on any side; the structure appears windowless from all views shown
attribute_door_placement: Single arched entrance centered on the front facade, supported by wooden posts
